title: Motor Fault
shortDescription: The washer motor has encountered an electrical fault.
fullDescription: The E02 error indicates a motor malfunction. The motor control module has detected an abnormal condition such as a short circuit, open circuit, or current overload in the motor windings. The washer will not operate until the motor issue is resolved.
symptoms: Motor hums but does not spin
Error displayed at start of cycle

fixSteps.title: Reset the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the washer, unplug it for 5 minutes, then plug it back in and try a cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check motor brushes
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the rear panel. The carbon brushes are located on either side of the motor. Remove each brush and check length - replace if shorter than 15mm.

fixSteps.title: Inspect motor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Check the wiring connections between the motor and the control module. Reseat any loose connectors.

fixSteps.title: Test the motor
fixSteps.instruction: Use a multimeter to test the motor windings for continuity and check for shorts to ground. Replace the motor if windings are faulty.

whenToCallPro: If the motor or motor control module needs replacement, or if brush replacement does not resolve the issue.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens washer E02 motor error fix

faq.q: How long do motor brushes last?
faq.a: Motor carbon brushes typically last 5-8 years depending on usage. Heavy use shortens their lifespan.

faq.q: Are Siemens and Bosch motor parts interchangeable?
faq.a: Many parts are interchangeable since both brands are manufactured by BSH. Always verify the part number for your specific model.

faq.q: Can I replace the brushes myself?
faq.a: Yes, brush replacement is a common DIY repair. The brushes slide out from holders on the sides of the motor.

title: Motor Tachometer Error
shortDescription: The motor speed sensor (tachometer) is not providing a signal.
fullDescription: The E04 error code indicates the motor tachometer (speed sensor) is not sending speed information to the control board. The tachometer is a small sensor on the motor that tells the control board how fast the motor is spinning. Without this feedback, the washer cannot control drum speed properly.

fixSteps.title: Reset the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes, plug back in, and test with a spin cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check tachometer connection
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the motor from the rear panel. Locate the tachometer connector on the motor and ensure it is firmly seated.

fixSteps.title: Test the tachometer
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the tachometer wires and test resistance with a multimeter. It should read approximately 115-170 ohms. Replace if significantly different.

fixSteps.title: Replace tachometer or motor
fixSteps.instruction: If the tachometer has failed, it may be replaceable separately or the entire motor may need replacement depending on the model.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the tachometer does not fix the issue and the motor control module is suspected.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens washer E04 tachometer speed sensor fix

faq.q: Where is the tachometer on a Siemens washer?
faq.a: The tachometer is a small circular sensor attached to the rear of the motor, usually with a plug connector.

faq.q: Can I test the tachometer without removing the motor?
faq.a: Yes, you can access the tachometer connector from the rear of the washer and test it in place.

faq.q: Is E04 the same on Siemens and Bosch?
faq.a: Yes, Siemens and Bosch use the same error code system as they share the same BSH platform.

title: Water Fill Timeout
shortDescription: The washer did not fill with water within the expected time.
fullDescription: The E13 error indicates the washer has been trying to fill with water but the water level has not reached the required height within the timeout period. Water may not be entering at all or entering too slowly.
symptoms: Washer does not fill with water
Long delay at beginning of cycle

fixSteps.title: Check water taps
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure both hot and cold water taps behind the washer are fully open.

fixSteps.title: Check inlet hoses
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect both inlet hoses for kinks, bends, or damage. Straighten any kinks.

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet filters
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off water supply, disconnect hoses, and clean the mesh filter screens at the inlet valve connections.

fixSteps.title: Test inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: With the washer unplugged, test the inlet valve solenoid coils with a multimeter for continuity. Replace if any coil reads open.

whenToCallPro: If the inlet valve needs replacement or if home water pressure is too low.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens washer E13 water fill timeout fix

faq.q: Is E13 the same as F17?
faq.a: Both relate to water fill issues. E13 is a fill timeout, while F17 specifically indicates fill time exceeded.

faq.q: What water pressure does a Siemens washer need?
faq.a: Siemens washers require a minimum water pressure of 1 bar (approximately 15 PSI).

faq.q: Can a water softener affect filling?
faq.a: During regeneration, a water softener can reduce pressure. If E13 occurs intermittently, check if it coincides with softener cycles.

title: Fill Time Exceeded
shortDescription: Water is entering too slowly and fill time has been exceeded.
fullDescription: The E17/F17 error code indicates the washer detected water is entering but too slowly. The required water level was not reached within the maximum fill time. Unlike E13 which may indicate no water at all, E17 specifically means water is flowing but not fast enough.
symptoms: Cycle takes very long to start

fixSteps.title: Fully open water taps
fixSteps.instruction: Turn both taps fully counterclockwise to ensure maximum water flow.

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet filters
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off taps, disconnect hoses, and clean the small mesh screens at both the hose ends and the washer inlet valve ports.

fixSteps.title: Check water pressure
fixSteps.instruction: Test your mains water pressure. Siemens requires minimum 1 bar. If other appliances have low flow at the same time, pressure may be insufficient.

fixSteps.title: Replace inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: If filters are clean and pressure is adequate, the valve may not be opening fully. Replace the water inlet valve.

whenToCallPro: If home water pressure is chronically low or the inlet valve needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens washer E17 F17 slow fill error fix

faq.q: Why is my Siemens washer filling slowly?
faq.a: The most common causes are partially closed taps, clogged inlet screens, or low mains water pressure.

faq.q: Can I use the washer with low water pressure?
faq.a: The washer may still operate but will take longer to fill and may trigger E17. A booster pump can help if mains pressure is consistently low.

faq.q: Are E17 and F17 the same?
faq.a: Yes, the washer may display either code depending on the model and firmware version.

title: Drain Time Exceeded
shortDescription: The washer has exceeded the maximum allowed drain time.
fullDescription: The E18/F18 error code indicates the washer was unable to drain the water within the allowed time. The drain pump may be blocked, the drain hose may be clogged, or the pump has failed. The washer will stop the cycle with water still in the drum.
symptoms: Drain pump runs but no water exits

fixSteps.title: Clean the drain pump filter
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the drain pump access panel at the lower front. Place towels and a shallow container below. Open the filter cap slowly to drain water, then remove and clean the filter.

fixSteps.title: Check the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the drain hose for kinks or blockages. Disconnect from the standpipe and check for clogs. The hose should be installed between 60-100cm height.

fixSteps.title: Check pump impeller
fixSteps.instruction: With the filter removed, look inside the pump housing and check if the impeller rotates freely. Remove any coins, buttons, or debris.

fixSteps.title: Replace drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: If the pump motor is not running, disconnect power, access the pump from the front lower area, disconnect the hoses and wiring, and install a new pump.

whenToCallPro: If the drain pump motor has failed and needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens washer E18 F18 drain error fix

faq.q: How do I access the drain pump filter?
faq.a: Open the small access door at the lower front of the washer. The filter cap unscrews counterclockwise.

faq.q: Why does my Siemens washer keep getting E18?
faq.a: If E18 recurs frequently, you may have a chronic blockage issue. Check pockets before washing and clean the filter monthly.

faq.q: Is E18 the same as F18?
faq.a: Yes, both indicate a drain timeout. Different models may display either code.

title: Aquastop / Leak Detection Activated
shortDescription: The Aquastop leak protection system has been triggered.
fullDescription: The E23 error code indicates the Aquastop leak detection system has detected water in the base pan of the washer. This means there is an internal water leak. The washer will immediately stop filling and activate the drain pump to remove water. The Aquastop system protects against flooding from internal leaks.
symptoms: Drain pump runs immediately at startup

fixSteps.title: Turn off water and power
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the water supply taps and unplug the washer immediately to prevent further leaking.

fixSteps.title: Check for visible leaks
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the washer forward and check underneath for water. Look at all visible hose connections and the door boot seal for signs of leaking.

fixSteps.title: Inspect internal hoses
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the top and side panels to access internal hoses. Check all hose clamps and connections for drips or looseness.

fixSteps.title: Dry the base pan and reset
fixSteps.instruction: Tilt the washer back carefully and soak up water from the base pan. Dry the Aquastop float sensor. If no active leak was found, the sensor may have triggered from a small previous leak.

whenToCallPro: If the leak source cannot be identified or if the tub is cracked.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens washer E23 Aquastop leak detection fix

faq.q: What is Aquastop?
faq.a: Aquastop is a leak protection system used in Siemens and Bosch washers that detects water in the base pan and shuts off water supply to prevent flooding.

faq.q: Can the Aquastop sensor give false alarms?
faq.a: Yes, residual moisture or condensation in the base pan can sometimes trigger the sensor. Drying the base pan may clear the error.

faq.q: Do I need to replace the Aquastop hose?
faq.a: Only if the Aquastop inlet hose itself has failed. The hose has a built-in shut-off valve that activates if it detects a hose leak.

title: Pressure Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The water level pressure sensor (analog) has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: The E26 error code indicates the analog pressure sensor (water level sensor) has failed or is providing readings outside the expected range. This sensor monitors the water level in the tub. Without accurate readings, the washer cannot fill to the correct level or detect drain completion.

causes.cause: Blocked or kinked pressure sensor hose

symptoms: Cycle does not progress past fill

fixSteps.title: Check pressure hose
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the top panel. Locate the small rubber pressure hose running from the tub to the pressure sensor. Check for kinks, blockages, or disconnections.

fixSteps.title: Blow through the hose
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the pressure hose from the sensor. Gently blow through it to check for blockages. You should feel minimal resistance.

fixSteps.title: Test the pressure sensor
fixSteps.instruction: With the hose disconnected from the sensor, gently blow into the hose connection on the sensor. You should hear clicks as the switch triggers at different pressures.

fixSteps.title: Replace the pressure sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor does not click or provides incorrect readings, replace it. Remove the mounting clip, disconnect the hose and wiring, and install the new sensor.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the sensor does not resolve the issue, the control board may need diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens washer E26 pressure sensor fix

faq.q: What does the pressure sensor do?
faq.a: The pressure sensor measures the water level in the tub by detecting air pressure in a small hose connected to the tub.

faq.q: Can a blocked hose cause overfilling?
faq.a: Yes, if the pressure hose is blocked, the sensor cannot detect the water level and the washer may overfill.

faq.q: Where is the pressure sensor located?
faq.a: It is typically located at the top of the washer, near the rear panel, with a small rubber hose connecting it to the tub.

title: Flow Meter Error
shortDescription: The water flow meter is not detecting water flow correctly.
fullDescription: The E29 error code indicates the flow meter (water meter) in the inlet system is not detecting water flow as expected. The flow meter measures the volume of water entering the washer. If it fails, the washer cannot accurately control water levels.

causes.cause: Faulty inlet valve not allowing flow

fixSteps.title: Reset the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes, plug back in, and test a cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check flow meter connections
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the flow meter (located near the inlet valve). Ensure the electrical connector is secure.

fixSteps.title: Clean the flow meter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the flow meter and check for debris or scale buildup that could block the internal impeller.

fixSteps.title: Replace the flow meter
fixSteps.instruction: If cleaning does not fix the issue, replace the flow meter. Disconnect the water line, remove the old meter, and install the replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the flow meter and inlet valve are both functioning but the error persists.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens washer E29 flow meter error fix

faq.q: What is a flow meter?
faq.a: A flow meter measures the volume of water entering the washer, allowing precise control of water levels for each cycle.

faq.q: Can hard water damage the flow meter?
faq.a: Yes, hard water can cause scale buildup on the flow meter impeller, reducing accuracy or causing failure.

faq.q: Do all Siemens washers have a flow meter?
faq.a: Most modern Siemens front-load washers include a flow meter for precise water dosing.

title: Door Not Closed
shortDescription: The washer door is not properly closed or the door switch is faulty.
fullDescription: The F16 error code indicates the washer control board has detected that the door is not closed. The door switch is not making contact. This can be caused by the door not being fully latched, a faulty door switch, or a broken door handle/latch mechanism.

fixSteps.title: Close the door firmly
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door fully and close it with a firm push until you hear the latch click.

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the door latch area and the door boot seal for any items preventing proper closure.

fixSteps.title: Test the door interlock
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Use a multimeter to test the door interlock switch for continuity when the door is closed.

fixSteps.title: Replace door interlock
fixSteps.instruction: If the interlock is faulty, remove the door boot seal retaining band, pull back the seal, remove the interlock screws, and install a new one.

whenToCallPro: If the door latch mechanism or interlock needs replacement and you are not comfortable removing the door boot seal.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens washer F16 door not closed error fix

faq.q: What does F16 mean on Siemens washer?
faq.a: F16 means the control board thinks the door is not closed. Check the door latch and interlock switch.

faq.q: Where is the door interlock?
faq.a: The door interlock is located behind the door boot seal on the right side of the door opening, secured by screws.

faq.q: Can a broken handle cause F16?
faq.a: Yes, if the handle is broken and cannot engage the latch mechanism, the door switch will not activate.

title: Water Inlet Timeout
shortDescription: Water is not filling fast enough within the allowed time.
fullDescription: The F17 error code means the water fill time has been exceeded. Water may be entering the washer but too slowly to reach the required level within the timeout period. This is the same issue as E17 displayed with a different code format depending on the model.

causes.cause: Water supply taps not fully open

symptoms: Long wait at start of cycle

fixSteps.title: Open water taps fully
fixSteps.instruction: Check both water taps behind the washer are fully open.

fixSteps.title: Clean inlet screens
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off taps, disconnect hoses, clean the filter screens at the hose connections and inlet valve.

fixSteps.title: Check water pressure
fixSteps.instruction: Test mains pressure at a nearby tap. Minimum required is 1 bar (15 PSI).

fixSteps.title: Replace inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: If screens are clean and pressure is adequate, the inlet valve may be restricting flow. Replace it.

whenToCallPro: If the problem is low mains pressure that cannot be resolved, or if the valve needs professional replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens washer F17 water inlet timeout fix

faq.q: Is F17 the same as E17?
faq.a: Yes, they indicate the same issue. Different models display different code formats.

faq.q: Can running other appliances cause F17?
faq.a: Yes, running multiple water-using appliances simultaneously can reduce pressure enough to trigger F17.

faq.q: What if only hot or cold is affected?
faq.a: If only one temperature is slow, that specific tap or inlet valve coil may be the issue.

title: Drain Timeout
shortDescription: The washer has exceeded the maximum drain time.
fullDescription: The F18 error code means the washer could not drain the water within the allowed time. This is the same as E18 with a different display format. The drain pump may be blocked, the drain hose clogged, or the pump has failed.

fixSteps.title: Clean drain pump filter
fixSteps.instruction: Open the service flap at the lower front. Place towels and container below. Slowly open the filter cap and clean out debris.

fixSteps.title: Check drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the drain hose for kinks. Check the connection to the standpipe for blockages.

fixSteps.title: Check pump impeller
fixSteps.instruction: With the filter removed, shine a flashlight into the pump housing and rotate the impeller by hand to ensure it moves freely.

fixSteps.title: Replace drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: If the pump does not run, access it from the front lower area, disconnect wiring and hoses, and install a new pump.

whenToCallPro: If the drain pump has failed and needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens washer F18 drain timeout fix

faq.q: Is F18 the same as E18?
faq.a: Yes, same drain timeout issue, different display format depending on the model.

faq.q: How often should I clean the filter?
faq.a: Clean the drain pump filter at least every 2-3 months, or monthly for heavy use.

faq.q: Can coins damage the drain pump?
faq.a: Yes, coins and small metal objects can jam or damage the pump impeller. Always check pockets before washing.

title: Heating Timeout
shortDescription: The water is not reaching the target temperature in the allowed time.
fullDescription: The F19 error code indicates the water in the washer did not reach the target temperature within the allowed heating time. The heating element may have failed, or the temperature sensor (NTC) may be providing incorrect readings.

fixSteps.title: Run a descale cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Run a hot wash cycle (90C) with a descaling agent or citric acid to remove any scale buildup from the heating element.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the heating element from the rear or front panel. Disconnect the wires and test for continuity (should read 20-40 ohms). Also test for continuity to ground (should be none).

fixSteps.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: The NTC sensor is usually on or near the heating element. Disconnect and test resistance (should be approximately 5,000 ohms at room temperature).

fixSteps.title: Replace faulty component
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the heating element or NTC sensor as identified by testing.

whenToCallPro: If both the element and sensor test fine but the error persists, the control board relay may need professional testing.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens washer F19 heating timeout error fix

faq.q: Can scale cause heating problems?
faq.a: Yes, heavy limescale buildup on the element insulates it and reduces heating efficiency. Regular descaling helps prevent this.

faq.q: Where is the heating element?
faq.a: On most Siemens front-load washers, the heating element is accessible from the rear panel at the bottom of the tub.

faq.q: What is an NTC sensor?
faq.a: NTC stands for Negative Temperature Coefficient. It is a temperature sensor whose resistance decreases as temperature increases.

title: Unexpected Heating
shortDescription: The washer is heating water when it should not be.
fullDescription: The F20 error code indicates the washer has detected an unexpected rise in water temperature. The heater is activating when it should be off, or the temperature is rising without the heater being commanded on. This is a safety issue that could indicate a relay stuck in the on position.

causes.cause: Stuck relay on the control board

symptoms: Water too hot for selected cycle
Steam or excessive heat from washer

fixSteps.title: Power off immediately
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer immediately. Unexpected heating is a safety concern.

fixSteps.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power and test the NTC sensor resistance. At room temperature it should read approximately 5,000 ohms. A significantly different reading means it is faulty.

fixSteps.title: Test heating element to ground
fixSteps.instruction: Check the heating element for continuity to ground (earth). There should be NO continuity. If there is, the element is shorted and must be replaced.

fixSteps.title: Replace control board if relay stuck
fixSteps.instruction: If the NTC and element test fine, the heater relay on the control board is likely stuck on. Replace the main control board.

whenToCallPro: This is a safety issue. If the cause is a stuck relay on the control board, professional repair is strongly recommended.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens washer F20 unexpected heating error fix

faq.q: Is F20 dangerous?
faq.a: Yes, unexpected heating can overheat water and damage fabrics. Do not use the washer until the cause is found and fixed.

faq.q: Can I still use cold wash cycles?
faq.a: No, do not use the washer at all until F20 is resolved, as the heater may activate regardless of the selected temperature.

faq.q: What causes a relay to stick?
faq.a: Age, power surges, or component failure can cause the heater relay to weld shut, keeping the heater constantly on.

title: Motor / Drum Rotation Error
shortDescription: The drum is not rotating correctly or the motor has a fault.
fullDescription: The F21 error code indicates the control board attempted to rotate the drum but the motor did not respond correctly. The drum may not spin at all, or may not reach the target speed. This can be caused by motor brush wear, a faulty motor, or mechanical resistance in the drum.
symptoms: Motor hums but drum is stationary

fixSteps.title: Check for drum obstruction
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door and try to rotate the drum by hand. It should turn smoothly. Listen for scraping sounds.

fixSteps.title: Check motor brushes
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the rear panel. Locate the motor and check the carbon brushes. Replace if shorter than 15mm.

fixSteps.title: Check the drive belt
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the drive belt for wear, cracks, or breakage. The belt runs from the motor pulley around the drum.

fixSteps.title: Test the motor
fixSteps.instruction: If brushes are good, test the motor windings with a multimeter for continuity and test for shorts to ground.

whenToCallPro: If the motor or motor control module needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens washer F21 motor drum rotation error fix

faq.q: Are motor brushes a common issue?
faq.a: Yes, worn motor brushes are one of the most common causes of F21 on Siemens washers. They are inexpensive and relatively easy to replace.

faq.q: How do I know if the brushes are worn?
faq.a: Remove them from the motor. If they are shorter than 15mm or unevenly worn, they need replacement.

faq.q: Can an overloaded washer cause F21?
faq.a: Yes, an extremely heavy load can prevent the motor from reaching the target speed. Try reducing the load.

title: Aquastop Activated / Leak Detected
shortDescription: The Aquastop system has detected a water leak.
fullDescription: The F23 error code means the Aquastop leak detection system has been activated. Water has been detected in the washer's base pan, triggering the flood protection. The washer will stop filling and activate the drain pump. This is similar to E23 but uses the F-code format.
symptoms: Washer will not fill or run

fixSteps.title: Stop water and power
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off water taps and unplug the washer.

fixSteps.title: Check for leaks
fixSteps.instruction: Look under and behind the washer for water. Check the door seal, inlet hoses, and drain connections.

fixSteps.title: Inspect base pan
fixSteps.instruction: Carefully tilt the washer back and dry any water in the base pan. Check the Aquastop float for proper movement.

fixSteps.title: Find and fix the leak
fixSteps.instruction: Remove panels and trace the leak source. Tighten loose connections, replace damaged hoses, or replace the door seal as needed.

whenToCallPro: If the leak cannot be found or is coming from the tub.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens washer F23 Aquastop leak fix

faq.q: Is F23 the same as E23?
faq.a: Yes, both indicate Aquastop activation. Different models display different code formats.

faq.q: Can I reset Aquastop without fixing the leak?
faq.a: You can dry the base pan to clear the sensor, but the code will return if the leak is not fixed.

faq.q: How does the Aquastop inlet hose work?
faq.a: The Aquastop hose has an electric valve that opens only when the washer needs water. If the hose detects a leak, the valve stays closed.

title: Door Lock Not Engaging
shortDescription: The door lock mechanism is not engaging properly.
fullDescription: The F34 error code indicates the door lock solenoid has not engaged within the expected time. The control board sent the lock command but the lock did not confirm engagement. This is different from F16 (door not closed) as F34 specifically indicates the lock mechanism failure rather than the door being open.
symptoms: F34 displayed at start of cycle

fixSteps.title: Reset the washer
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes, plug back in, and try starting a cycle.

fixSteps.title: Check door alignment
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the door closes properly and the striker aligns with the lock mechanism. Adjust hinges if needed.

fixSteps.title: Test the door lock
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the door boot retaining band and pull back the seal to access the lock. Check the wiring connections. Test the lock solenoid with a multimeter.

fixSteps.title: Replace the door lock
fixSteps.instruction: If the lock solenoid is faulty, remove the mounting screws, disconnect the wiring, and install a new lock.

whenToCallPro: If the lock and wiring are in good condition but the error persists, indicating a control board issue.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens washer F34 door lock error fix

faq.q: What is the difference between F16 and F34?
faq.a: F16 means the door is not closed. F34 means the door is closed but the lock mechanism is not engaging.

faq.q: Can I open the door during F34?
faq.a: Yes, since the lock is not engaging, the door should open normally.

faq.q: How long should the lock take to engage?
faq.a: The door lock should engage within a few seconds of pressing Start. If you hear clicking but no lock, the solenoid may be weak.

title: Motor Not Turning / Drum Blocked
shortDescription: The motor is unable to turn the drum.
fullDescription: The F43 error code indicates the motor control has completely failed to rotate the drum. This is a more severe version of F21 where no rotation at all is detected. The drum may be mechanically blocked, the motor may have completely failed, or the motor control module may be defective.

causes.cause: Drum mechanically blocked by foreign object
Failed motor or motor control module

symptoms: Drum cannot be turned by hand (if mechanically blocked)

fixSteps.title: Check for drum obstruction
fixSteps.instruction: Try to rotate the drum by hand. If it does not move, something is blocking it. Check between the drum and the door seal.

fixSteps.title: Check motor brushes
fixSteps.instruction: If the drum moves freely by hand, inspect the motor brushes. Completely worn brushes (less than 5mm) will prevent any rotation.

fixSteps.title: Test motor electrically
fixSteps.instruction: Use a multimeter to test the motor windings and check that the control module is providing power to the motor.

fixSteps.title: Replace motor or control module
fixSteps.instruction: Based on testing, replace the motor, motor brushes, or motor control module as needed.

whenToCallPro: If the motor or control module needs replacement, or if bearings have seized.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens washer F43 motor blocked drum error fix

faq.q: Is F43 more serious than F21?
faq.a: Yes, F43 indicates complete motor failure to rotate, while F21 may indicate partial or intermittent rotation issues.

faq.q: Can a bra wire cause F43?
faq.a: Yes, underwire from bras can get between the drum and tub, jamming the drum. Use a mesh laundry bag for bras.

faq.q: How do I access the motor?
faq.a: Remove the rear panel of the washer. The motor is at the bottom, connected to the drum by a drive belt.

title: Inverter/Motor Module Error
shortDescription: The motor inverter module has detected an internal fault.
fullDescription: The F57 error code indicates the motor inverter module (VFD - Variable Frequency Drive) has encountered a fault. This module controls motor speed on brushless motor models. The error may be caused by a failed inverter, motor winding issues, or a communication fault between the main control board and the inverter.
symptoms: F57 displayed at startup or during cycle

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer for 10 minutes to allow the inverter to fully discharge. Plug back in and test.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring connections
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the inverter module (usually at the back of the washer near the motor). Check all wiring connections between the inverter, motor, and main board.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for damage
fixSteps.instruction: Visually inspect the inverter module for burn marks, bulging capacitors, or corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Replace inverter module
fixSteps.instruction: If the module is damaged or the error persists, replace the inverter module. This is usually a plug-and-play replacement.

whenToCallPro: This is a complex electronic repair. Professional diagnosis is recommended to determine if the inverter or motor is at fault.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens washer F57 inverter motor module error fix

faq.q: What is the inverter module?
faq.a: The inverter controls the speed of brushless motors used in modern Siemens washers, allowing precise speed control.

faq.q: Does my washer have an inverter?
faq.a: Most modern Siemens iQ300, iQ500, and iQ700 models use inverter-driven brushless motors.

faq.q: Can a power surge damage the inverter?
faq.a: Yes, power surges can damage the inverter module electronics. Consider using a surge protector.

title: Software/Firmware Error
shortDescription: The washer control board has experienced a software error.
fullDescription: The F63 error code indicates the washer control board has encountered a software or firmware error. The processor may have locked up or experienced a data corruption. This error often resolves with a power reset but recurring F63 may indicate a control board failure.
symptoms: Random error at any point in cycle

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the washer for 10 minutes to allow a full capacitor discharge. Plug back in and test.

fixSteps.title: Check control board connections
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the top panel to access the control board. Check all connectors are firmly seated.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for damage
fixSteps.instruction: Look for visible damage on the control board including burn marks, corrosion, or damaged components.

fixSteps.title: Replace control board
fixSteps.instruction: If the error persists after multiple resets, the control board likely needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the error recurs after power resets, professional diagnosis is recommended.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens washer F63 software firmware error fix

faq.q: Is F63 a serious error?
faq.a: A single occurrence cleared by a power reset is not serious. Recurring F63 indicates a failing control board.

faq.q: Can a power surge cause F63?
faq.a: Yes, power surges can corrupt the firmware on the control board. Use a surge protector to prevent this.

faq.q: How much does a control board cost?
faq.a: Siemens washer control boards typically cost between $100 and $280 depending on the model.

title: Water Level Too High / Overfill
shortDescription: The washer has detected the water level is above the maximum safe level.
fullDescription: The F31 error code indicates the pressure sensor has detected that the water level in the tub has exceeded the maximum safe level. The washer will stop filling and activate the drain pump to remove excess water. This is typically caused by a stuck-open water inlet valve or a faulty pressure sensor.
symptoms: Washer fills when it should not

fixSteps.title: Turn off water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Immediately close both water taps behind the washer to stop water flow.

fixSteps.title: Check for stuck inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: With the washer unplugged but the water supply on, check if water continues to enter the tub. If it does, the valve is stuck open.

fixSteps.title: Check pressure sensor hose
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Remove the top panel and check the pressure sensor hose for kinks or blockages. A blocked hose prevents accurate water level detection.

fixSteps.title: Replace inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: If the valve is stuck open, disconnect power and water supply, remove the rear or top panel, disconnect the wiring and hoses, and install a new valve.

whenToCallPro: If the control board relay is stuck, or if the cause of overfilling cannot be determined.
videoSearchQuery: Siemens washer F31 overfill water level error fix

faq.q: Is F31 dangerous?
faq.a: Yes, overfilling can cause water damage. Turn off the water supply immediately when F31 appears.

faq.q: Can a faulty pressure sensor cause F31?
faq.a: Yes, if the sensor cannot detect the actual water level, the washer may fill past the safe level before the error triggers.

faq.q: Should I turn off the water taps between washes?
faq.a: While not required, turning off the taps between uses prevents potential flooding from a stuck valve.
